questions.text: Which of the following is one of the three structural properties that makes AI agents uniquely vulnerable compared to traditional software?
options: They have cross-system write scope combined with multi-step autonomy
Tricking users into typing malicious commands
Exploiting a bug in the model's training
Embedding malicious instructions in data the AI reads, not in the user's direct message
Modifying the AI's system prompt via the API

questions.text: In the 'confused-deputy' problem as it applies to AI agents, who is the deputy?
According to our test battery, which model tier was compromised by ALL attacks tested?
How many enterprise workflow attack categories are covered in this course?
